reviews.review:
KILL YOURSELF!!!!!!

10/10

This game is unbalanced as hell. It was fun at the first 2 hours but then it became annoying. Like why are there 100000 cops? I finish one fight then there's another one and I haven't moved a pixel. Also some enemies are waaaaay to tanky like the clowns, the discord mod and it's food, etc.. If I am near to kill one then it spawn another one in the fight LIKE WHY?! 

It would be so much better and fun if it would turn into a rage game and it would be balanced. 
You should add a difficulty option, that way if someone would like to play it and not sweat it's pants because they get one shotted by a fking cop or an addicted, they would enjoy it and have a laugh. 

As I said I enjoyed the first couple of hours and I would like to play it more but it just feels like wasting my time.. I will be back when there will be some changes..
reviews.developer_response: yeah, 100000 cops is a bit much. I'll have karen get them defunded next update

reviews.review:
Dude like, this has to be the most niche game I've ever played, but the way it's so bizarre and sloppy kinda hooked me in??? I still wouldn't recommend this to anyone, but I can't say it's just 100% dogshlt. I guess I just happened to be part of the target audience.
Sometimes it IS interesting to look at a piece of media and just think to yourself "what the fuck is this?" and "what went through the mind of the devs?" like you have some edgy toxic crabs at one point, horrific realistic meat slop at another, then some horrific mexican clowns?? and they all look horrible, but for this game, it's its own charm. Then two seconds later the main characters will have some cool dialog or things happening that aren't shltpost-y and gives me whiplash.

I played for 17 hours, most of that was the very, very slow combat, which there is a lot of in the game; and many of which I had to fight multiple times because the enemy instantly killed me (which was funny the first times, but it became frustrating) and I had to reload a save, and go through the same 3-4 long fights before reaching some boss enemy and testing my chances again. Yeah if it was just the combat alone I prob would have dropped it earlier, but I was very interested in the world the game has, the different lockers kinda make me feel like i'm in Yume Nikki exploring the different worlds, I love that aspect of the game, sometimes I just wanted to grab cheat engine or overlevel myself just so I could "skip" the battles and explore freely.
Now I stopped playing because it felt like I've seen most of the game so far and don't see a goal or where to go or what to do, having my last things done being [spoiler] defeating Midas, and exploring some hills with bell guys (killed two eldritch horrors to get no reward at all haha) [/spoiler]

dude why did I play this for 17 hours...
reviews.developer_response: Yeah you probably did see most of the game, it's still early access with no ending yet. Just moved it from itch to steam because I was paid $1k to do it

reviews.review:
I like the crappy visuals and the out-of-place music, but the banter is miserable.

In my first half-dozen combats, I had a crab tell me:

To Kill myself
That I was pathetic and it left the battle, granting me its XP (funny the first time, exhausting the next 12)
When it died it would haunt me, telling me to kill myself

Then the crab morphed into an Uber Crab and no matter how many times it duked me its XP and left, or I ran, I could never end the combat.

Easy uninstall, which is kind of a shame because I do think there's some neat stuff going on in here. I don't think I should have to put up with a Fornite lobby's worth of abuse to experience it - isn't there enough Fortnight lobby out there already? Perhaps the game creator could stand to tap someone else - hell, even ChatGPT - to come up with some better trash talk.
reviews.developer_response: The crab was never meant to be good at trash talk, also I'll fix the constantly being attacked by it

reviews.review: There is a warning about the type of humor. That being said, I've only started the game and I already love it so much. It's cool to see a game with the same ungodly humor. The music is such a vibe, I could listen to it all day. I can't wait to get through the whole game.

I got stuck in an endless loop fighting many crabs 🦀 since they would run away and instantly aggro again and again - unfortunately, I couldn't progress and so just decided to move on. 

I was simply going to not recommend it due to this but after meditating on this potentially life changing decision, I've decided to instead recommend it because;

a. It's not my type of game anyway (usually) being pixel graphics. It'd be unfair for me to not recommend this particular game just for this. The dark humour kept it interesting for a bit though tbf. 

b. I'm unlikely to ever play it again so won't know if this issue is fixed.

c. It [i] was [/i] FREE and even now the price being under <£5 shows that the dev isn't greedy. 

d. I'm in a good mood 

This pleases me. Nothing lost, nothing gained. For this reason I give it a thumbs up. 👍

- Game is buggy - in some areas like clipping out of bounds
- In the first section of the game, I though I could go through those secret areas but I realized that I can't after wasting like 1 hour checking everywhere
- Some parts of the game can lead to soft-lock [spoiler] like in the forest section where you have Haunted Heart in your party and if your entire team dies during the battle, the game keeps attacking the Heart even though it has like 9999999999 health bar and literally won’t die (even the attacks doesn't damages it). aaaaaaaaaa [/spoiler]
- Some enemies can be annoying as hell because some of their attacks are way too OP, which could f##k your whole party even if your teammates are strong enough for that area
- Please for the love of god, add a [u] return to menu[/u] button. I'm tired to resetting the game to load my previous save data
- There's a lack of deep between your sisters 
- [spoiler] After finishing The Forest section, I was a little confused about the death of "Catherine". The discussion around it felt really dry. Like "Vibia" didn't care at all. Also I was confused about where to go next. I had 4 lockers unlocked and almost all of them was too hard for me to progress and didn't know where should I go first [/spoiler]


buuuuuuuuuuuuuuuuuuuuuut
I still like this game because it's weird and unique compared to other typical RPG games. Like all the time game is trying to say to kill myself which I find it hilarious
And it's enjoyable if you have a lot of free time because it takes a while to progress (leveling up and enemy levels are messed up) especially in the beginning of this game but I'm okay with it
Also It reminds me of "fear and hunger" which is also a tough game too

[b] I F*@#NG HATE CRABS, BUT STILL, I LOVE THEM 🦀 [/b]

I would love to put good review, but game is so chaotic even in cotrnol, that i was not able to play it properly.
Mouse or keyboard, both is very lagging.

I CAN NOT RECOMMEND TO MY FRIEND NOR MY GRANDMA.
reviews.developer_response: if the game is lagging I suggest upgrading your dinosaur pc :steamhappy:

reviews.review:
Honestly, I'm not entirely sure where I stand on this game. A lot of it I enjoyed, and the story seems quite interesting, but the excessive cringe of the enemies (oddly placed against a less-than-crude story plot) also gets old pretty early on, and definitely pulls you away from it. 

The story also is a bit too light to give you a clear path forward. You'll pretty much just wander in circles, not knowing what to do, until you figure out where to head next. The variety of maps and settings is surprisingly nice though, so it's not a total misery to get lost. All the enemies respawn every time you leave the area, which is great for leveling up, and torturous if you're not looking to do so.  (Some police officers that respawn every single time you leave a house or shop, and which absolutely cannot be escaped, definitely come to mind.) 

Leveling up is also frustratingly vague. Its hard to know which characters actually benefit from certain areas being increased, as quite a few of the attacks/moves seem too ineffective to be worth it. I pretty much stuck to about 20% of the available moves once the characters were fully leveled, as the battles took way too long if I tried experimenting with other moves and strategies. Disappointing, as the game really does show a lot of different moves and has the potential to be a really fun experience there.

Also, I think the game developer might have some urine-based fetishes, given how often it comes up in game. Which is every other minute. I mean, its really a lot, even in terms of intentional cringe. It gets a little too gross and creepy there, that's for sure. 

Aaaanyways, I eventually I stepped away from the game, and I haven't been super inclined to try it again, not in it's current state. However, I did really enjoy playing it for a time, and especially enjoyed the sister characters and the world design, travel, and lore. At first, I didn't even mind at first that the enemies are so obviously, intentionally cringey. It just became a bit too much given how often you have to deal with them. Funny for a while, but it pulls away from the story too much in the end. 

This game definitely has a ton of potential, but I can't quite recommend it yet, not when it could actually be something legitimately good with a bit more time. Hoping the development with this continues, as there really is a lot going for it.
reviews.developer_response:
development is continuing, only put it on steam because someone paid me $1k to do it :D

If there's anything that makes it awful to play or just straight up broken feel free to leave a bug report (I read every discussion)

reviews.review: Game broke me before I broke it!
reviews.developer_response: how can you break a game that's already broken?

reviews.review:
I bought the game after seeing just a few screenshots. Some people may not like the style or how the game functions, but I can say I haven't played a game that actually made me laugh because of the dialogue. This game is clearly targeted toward people with a specific sense of humor, and I love the fever dream-esque theme. I'm always looking forward to what wild thing will happen next when I play. 

It is an old style RPG and whats so wrong with that? It adds to the charm. Controls are totally fine and there's no need to change it. Combat system is like any other RPG with some cool mechanics.

There is somewhat of a lack of enemy variety in the first two areas. It's funny, but it would really help if there were more variety.

The negative reviews boil down to people who're either:
A. Trash at video games
B. Clearly not the intended audience

It's pretty simple, If you love RPGs, funny and wild stuff, you'll enjoy yourself for sure.👍👍
reviews.developer_response: don't listen to the crabs, they're mean

reviews.review:
Let me tell you a story. It was a crisp Sunday morning.
I had just arrived home, and I was sittin out on my porch smokin a cigar.
It was the last cigar I had, so I wanted to make it count.
I was doin a pretty fine job of that until, for just a moment...
I let go of the cigar while it wasn't in my mouth.
The cigar fell out of my hand and on to the porch,
Where it rolled off into the grass.
Upon realising that I didn't have any more cigars...
I was mighty upset.
I didn't have enough time to hike 15 miles to the general store.
The thought of not havin a cigar simply terrified me.
I immediately fell into a deep depression,
Distraught that I wouldn't get to have a cigar.
Less than four hours later, in a fit of cigar withdrawal madness,
I pulled a silly stunt.
Grabbed my trusty piece, played russian roulette all by myself.
Till the grim reaper showed up, and poof!
Just like that, I waved bye-bye.
reviews.developer_response: don't smoke, that shit kills

reviews.review:
Well I have various thing to highlight about this game, all positive btw.

Easy and familiar game mechanics if you have played other more popular games before. Character designs are very original, well made and funni. Like who tf would imagine an Ubers' car wheels would be the eyes of a crab.

On the other hand, this games comforts me. I like how its a mix of absurdity of mature themes with a drip of serious lore.As a person who is dealing with thought stuff I find this a really good game concept which even reflects irl human behaivour. Lots of ppl tend to laugh at their own traumatic situations as a way to relief to avoid constant overwhelming.

Is it worth the money? 
I must admit that if I never saw a random local online article that promotes low price/ free games I would have never discovered Three Sisters. 
I obtained this game for free and must say that is worth paying for it keeping in mind such things as:
* Is a game made with dedication from an small team.
* Frequent updates/ bug fixes.
* More gameplay will come someday.

Overall I find this game kind of unique and entretaining. Its just a game with joking manners with no "depth" most of the times. This review is kinda long cuz I like to get philosophical about anything and try to find meanings where they dont exist lol.

Just a quick reminder, its not the games' fault if you ignored all the warnings and started crying cuz you double-clicked Enter after an Ubercrab tells you to kys.

PD: It would be cool to have point decorations, I want little Vibias all over my profile.
reviews.developer_response: Just a little correction for you, the "small team" is just one person who got funded by a Saudi oil baron to do whatever he wants :D

reviews.review: Honestly I want to try and play this game but it makes it almost impossible to do so.  I get to the overworld and try to avoid the ubercrabs, which are literally the first enemies of the game and yet right before I can kill them they give up and run away, which then leads me back to the overworld, where my character spawns on them and starts the battle, where they run away again.  It is impossible for me to progress, I can't level up, I can't beat them cause they run away before I can.  I literally can not progress past the first encounter.  I know its not a skill issue because I hit great on every attack, they just run away and instantly go back into battle.  If this gets fixed I will gladly try playing again.
reviews.developer_response: This was already on the list of stuff for me to fix, should be less hellish next update :steamhappy:

reviews.review: Goofy ahh game bruv

Gonna be honest, it's just not for me. The music sounded great so far. Reminded me of OFF a bit. The art looks neat (it's not AI generated for those wondering). Got past the tutorial. Fought a few enemies with only one attack with a skillbased "click when the circle is smallest" gimmick that didn't really change attack power or anything much. So basically just exchanged blows with intentionally annoying crabs that tell you to kys. One gave me a noose. haha nice. Get jumped by a gang of like 8 and are forced to fight them still having one attack. Nah, that sounds like a long slow and boring process. Use noose. Gameover. Realize there were no save points up until this point. Alt+F4. 

While I didn't have a good time with my time, I'm leaving a positive review because I know some will and for the price of free, try it.
reviews.developer_response: you get more attacks after like 10 minutes of grinding and exploring if that helps any. Hope you come back later on :steamhappy:
Yeah basically

reviews.review: It's one of those older style rpgs with awful controls, except usually that gets made up for by something unique or interesting. This game feels like if a 14 year old discovered what the internet was like in the early 2000's and decided to base their entire personality around it. Really? A crab that tells you to kill yourself? A "drugs and hookers" cave? How revolutionary. Its like you're trying to implement edgy "dark" humor into a horror game atmosphere, except you're doing a poor job with both of those themes. Like another review said, I got this game for free like everyone else did and I still feel ripped off.

reviews.review:
Pros :
I got this game for free during a promotion, but I think as it stands it's worth the 5 euros

The game is still in development, but already has a great story.


Cons :
The game is not for children because it contains dark humor, mainly about death, and AI-generated graphic horror, and you want to wait until your prefrontal cortex is developed before being exposed to this, to avoid it having consequences on your adult mind.

The game is very addictive. Your save points are far from the action, so it's really hard to stop playing.
reviews.developer_response:
Fun fact: it's not actually ai generated, I purposefully made some of the enemies look ai just to piss people off. :D

(It works btw, 10/10 would ragebait and gaslight people again)

reviews.review: I rarely leave rude reviews, but honestly, was this made by a teenager? I got incredible second-hand embarrassment from all the wannabe edgy "jokes" and terrible dialogue. Shit made me physically recoil from how bad it is. I know it's early access, but if this is how the game's dialogue is going to be in the full release, it's not going to be worth the price. I could not care less how many hours of content there is or how much time you put into this, none of that matters if the quality is absolute dogshit. The grammar and dialogue are horrible, battle system is garbage, nothing makes sense and the only enjoyable thing was closing the game. Early access does not mean it should be trash. I'm aware that they described it as "slop" and "shitpost" but if you're gonna make a game like that, at least make it fucking funny. I know the developer is literally just one person and I have no clue how old they are but their mental age is clearly 15 or lower.
reviews.developer_response: glad you enjoyed closing the game

reviews.review:
Greetings Traveler, 

you saw this game and though to yourself "lemme check those negative reviews and see whats up.." ?

Let me be your friendly guide!

Now i do have to make the disclaimer that i actually already wrote a review but was kindly asked to put it down since it got too detailed upon unpolished gameplay and other things- understandable, i take no offense to that... actually it is kinda cute? knowing that the Dev damn well is active and caring. I still want to give my unpopular opinion that nobody asked for but this time i try to make it feel less like a gametest/bug report.

Alrighty- 

The BIGGEST issue i have will be at the end so you do not get spoiled by my opinion while i try to be very objective through this review of the early access... and damn, there is content.

Here we FREAKI- is that crab telling me to hang myself?

Three SIsters is appearently a sequel to another Game, which i did not play because this is the first i got in contact with.. but what is it?

At its core (in my very wrong opionion) this is close to a classic JRPG- travel with your party through the world, collect key items and fight in a turnbased system (which is somewhat interactive) against your enemies! A
Also the soundtrack is great- but it is music and therefor you kinda have to like that kind of music? its a taste thingy... but i do like the soundtrack and appearently it will be released as DLC.

Here i have to make the comment that the combat is difficult, i will take a closer look at the individual characters and theire use later.

Exploring maps if you are patient and don't stress it is fun- assuming you like 2D pixelmap exploring alá FinalFantasy/GoldenSun.

The Story is there and i like it a lot so far. it isn't forced down your throat and it isn't consuming a lot of time which makes it a nice pace. The characters feel real enough that i can actually like them- no need to agree but like... or hate? approve. i approve of them.

The occasional Dungeon with the Bossfight and looting is there- and even if we are still early in development it gives enough to do some optimization.

now to my personal expirience with the game and i will not bother to hide spoilers- proceed on your own risk.

You start out with One char- Vibia- who can't do anything but swing her Spear

Your Attacks have to be timed with a ring which will be rated and effective accordingly- a neat twist to the rather flat Pixel-JRPG's you usually encounter.

Spells later on have a button mash mechanic for their potency- again, not reinventing the wheel but it feels different and makes you more invested!

Here i have to make a little disclaimer that the first Part of the game is ROUGH! not only because you probably don't know the game but also because of something called "Action economy" usually known among Pen & Paper players. but once you get over the hurdle you feel like you are playing the game... or against the game? its challanging you alll the time.

After beating the first hurdle you get your other two Partymembers:
Lucia- who seemse to be crying on her splashart even when smiling
Catherine- damn she is sassy.

Lucia is providing support spells and  she is very usefull for exploring maps, there are some nice items for her and she feels like the most sane person!

Cathrine is the chlichee white mage with heals, revives and let's you know that you are screwed without her, she has also the ability to annoy enemies. 
her res-spell heals the target to full hp! which is very good to keep you from reloading saves.
she does not have any noteworthy equipment and i have to admit there isn't anything worth giving her- not even the books that increase your Mind.

There is also "Extra" characters and some of them are weird...

now to stats which you can boost as you want by applying skill points- you get 15 per Lv and as of right now you make the decision what to focus fairly early.

this makes the early game after aquiring the three sisters (hehe, he said the title) easy but at somepoint (mexico) you just struggle.

I invested most of my points in HP except for Cathy because here revive is expensive.

Exploring is nice and all, and a little bit of puzzling is also aviable involving backtracking- hate it or love it, i love it.

The shop is neat, but there are items i can't figure out for the life of me.

The lack of savepoints is understandable if you want a hard game but be prepared to be suddenly steamrolled if you venture too far.

now to the biggest issue i have with this game and why it is so disgusting to me...

i never installed it.

this may sound like some ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ creepy pasta ♥♥♥♥ but it is the truth...

I never saw the storepage before, i never installed it... it just was there when i opened steam- already installed...

Is this a sign? are we living in a Simulation? Why is there a Stripper in the Crabcave?

Seriously tho... it creeped me out and that is the only reason why i don't recommend this game- anything else (assuming you like the genre and the crude humor) is top!
reviews.developer_response: The game is still highly underdeveloped, some of your complaints are already being addressed in future updates. Also, yes, there is a jump item in the game that you missed. If you wouldn't mind removing this review and moving it to a bug report discussion that would be greatly appreciated. I do plan on fixing things.

reviews.review: If you're a fan of crazy RPG Maker titles with bizarre visuals and humor that feels like it's taken from the pages of It's Always Sunny in Philadelphia, this is the game you need to check out. The combat is fun and engaging, allowing you to go at it at your own pace, and the music is a medley of different styles and flavors, appropriate for every situation. The story is interesting and thought-provoking, while also having plenty of humor through one-liners and witty dialogue between characters. If you've picked this up for free, then all the better for you. If not, it's only $5, and I'm sure you've got a Lincoln sitting in your pocket to throw at this game. It's definitely worth a shot!
reviews.developer_response: I agree, give me money so I can feed myself :steamhappy:

reviews.review:
I am so sorry, presumably young rpg maker developer, i wanted to love your quirky game, but it is not only dogwater but also boring.
If it were at least an entertaining kind of awful I could get behind it.
reviews.developer_response:
you do see the "Early Access" tag, right? The game is far from done, I just put it on Steam recently because people invested money into it and if anything happens at least it won't become lost media.

Also "young dev" is far from the truth, I've been making games for about 7-8 years now. This game is highly underdeveloped compared to others with only 600 hours of work put into it.

Sorry if getting pissed on by a hobo and ripping his spine out of his back out of anger just to sell it for some crack is too boring to you, I still plan on fleshing out the game more over time.

reviews.review:
I found this game via an article on gamerant that was more about Steam having great prices and less about the game itself, and having played it for a bit I'm reasonably sure that this is a case of the game breaking containment. having looked quickly through the reviews, I saw no mention made of the article, which left a bad enough taste in my mouth that i felt the need to write.

Three Sisters reminds me of Toilet in Wonderland and the LISA series in the unapologetic presentation of its topics and the addicting nature of its game play. To fans of games like those, i would recommend this game highly.

Edit: it was absolutely intended as a compliment, dev!
reviews.developer_response: LISA and Toilet in Wonderland were actually huge inspirations so I will take that as a great compliment!

ELEPHANT IN THE ROOM
The dev has used generative AI to create assets used in multiple sprites. Honestly sours the experience for a game that had a very interesting style.

Three sisters feels like a cheap knock off of hylics with a crass sense of humour. It held my interest for about 2 hours but the combat balance and AI art just ruins it. Ill return and see what the game becomes after full release but as for right now this game isnt even worth it for free.hi!

reviews.review:
[h1][b]Since nobody will read my review, here's a simple and basic chicken noodle soup recipe:[/b][/h1]

[h2]Ingredients:[/h2]
2 tablespoons olive oil or butter
1 small onion, chopped
2 carrots, sliced
2 celery stalks, sliced
3 cloves garlic, minced
8 cups chicken broth (or water with bouillon)
2 cups cooked shredded chicken (rotisserie or boiled)
2 cups egg noodles (or any pasta)
1 teaspoon salt (adjust to taste)
½ teaspoon black pepper
½ teaspoon dried thyme (optional)
½ teaspoon dried parsley (optional)

[h2]Instructions:[/h2]
[b]1. Sauté the Vegetables[/b]
• Heat oil or butter in a large pot over medium heat.
• Add onion, carrots, and celery.
• Cook for about 5 minutes until softened.
• Add garlic and cook for another minute.

[b]2. Add the Broth[/b]
• Pour in the chicken broth.
• Bring to a boil, then reduce the heat to a simmer.

[b]3. Cook the Noodles[/b]
• Add the noodles to the pot and cook according to package instructions (usually about 6-8 minutes) until tender.

[b]4. Add the Chicken[/b]
• Stir in the shredded chicken and season with salt, pepper, thyme, and parsley.
• Let it simmer for another 5 minutes.

[b]5. Serve[/b]
• Taste and adjust seasoning if needed.
• Serve hot and enjoy!

[h3]‪‪❤️❤️❤️ Please consider giving an award if you will cook this dish for your friends and loved ones. ❤️❤️❤️[/h3]
reviews.developer_response: thanks, I appreciate this more than a review :D
